mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in residential construction (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weak demand for durable goods, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: Interaction effect loses significance (p > 0.05) in 3-year rolling regressions, or 60d returns show positive correlation with combined RSAFS/HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through inflationary pressure and earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 55%+ of INDPRO contraction periods, or MA crossover strategy fails to outperform buy-and-hold (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through equity valuation compression.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of high CPI periods, or CPI's predictive power collapses in rolling window tests (IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain above 200k signals strong labor market demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through corporate revenue optimism and wage inflation expectations.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns in 55%+ of high payroll growth periods, or PAYEMS loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 3% indicates robust economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings and investment demand.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of high GDP growth periods, or GDP's predictive power vanishes in rolling window analysis (IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 6-month peak signals strong supply-side momentum, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate margins and sectoral growth.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in 55%+ of INDPRO breakout periods, or breakout strategy underperforms baseline (p > 0.1).
